purpose: The commit adds a check in hci_cmd_sync_queue to return an error when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing commands from being queued after the device has been unregistered and avoiding a potential use‑after‑free.

The commit removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h from the file n_hdlc.c. Since linux/tty.h already pulls in termios.h, the extra include is redundant and potentially confusing. The change cleans up the header list and simplifies the dependency graph.
The commit updates the documentation for the 'N/A' metric that was added for store operations in the perf c2c subsystem, reflecting changes in the report table.

This commit fixes race conditions and double‑free bugs in the hugetlb VMA lock handling during VMA splitting and range unmapping. It ensures that a VMA lock is allocated for every VM_MAYSHARE VMA, updates the lock copying logic in vm_area_dup(), and changes the unmap and sharing code to correctly free or prevent sharing of VMA locks, thereby preventing future bugs.

The commit adds documentation for two new power supply properties (AVDD and MICVDD) to the device tree binding of the Realtek rt5682s codec, enabling these supplies to be specified in device tree configurations.
Adds a missing iounmap() call in zorro7xx_remove_one() to free memory that was mapped by ioremap() during probe error handling, thereby eliminating a resource leak.

The commit removes an unused field named 'work' from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the mlx5e kTLS implementation. This change cleans up the data structure, eliminating dead code and potentially reducing memory usage or simplifying future maintenance.

The commit changes the type of the key_idx field in the cfg80211_auth_request structure from unsigned to signed. This is done to avoid static analysis warnings (e.g., from smatch) that arise when the field can be assigned a value of -1 to indicate that the key is not used. The change ensures the field can represent the required range of -1 to 3.
The commit reorders the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS list in the RISC-V defconfig files to match the ordering used by savedefconfig, without altering any functional behavior.

The commit introduces a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to encapsulate the logic that checks whether the MCP251XFD chip’s FIFOs are configured for CAN‑FD mode. It replaces multiple inline checks throughout the driver with calls to this new function, improving code readability and maintainability.
The commit modifies the RTC selftests to skip them when no RTC device is present, avoiding unnecessary test failures.

The commit removes a redundant assignment to the variable 'bytes' in ALSA echoaudio's midi.c. The variable is assigned a value that is never read before being overwritten in an if block, so the assignment is dead code. The change eliminates a clang dead-store warning and cleans up the source.

The commit changes the kernel configuration logic so that the rcuscale module can be built without automatically enabling RCU Tasks. Previously, when CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y, the kernel would always enable RCU Tasks unless rcuscale was built, which caused unnecessary code inclusion and increased complexity. This patch decouples the two, allowing rcuscale to be used independently, reducing kernel size and simplifying tracing operations.
The commit removes a duplicated "be split" string in the KVM x86 MMU header file spte.h, cleaning up redundant code.

The commit reverts a previous change that populated the dcn315 clock table using dcfclk values. Because the PMFW firmware fills the table when dcfclk states are disabled, the dcfclk-based table caused a read underflow. The revert restores the original behavior to avoid this underflow until a better solution is implemented.
The commit adds a new compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC (R9A07G054) to the Mali Bifrost GPU device tree bindings, enabling the kernel to recognize and configure this SoC's Mali-G31 GPU.

The commit renames the struct previously called `pcie_port` to `dw_pcie_rp` in the dwc PCIe core driver. This change aligns the struct name with the existing naming convention that prefixes driver-specific types with "dw_" and suffixes endpoint-related types with "_ep", improving consistency and readability across the codebase.

The commit changes the format string used in a netdev_dbg call for the AMD XGBE driver. The original format string "Protocol: %#06hx\n" incorrectly specifies a 16‑bit unsigned short (%hx) while the argument, after integer promotion, is an int. The patch replaces the format specifier with the promoted type’s flag (%x) to match the promoted argument type, eliminating the clang -Wformat warning.

The commit corrects a signed comparison bug in the MIPS jump_label branch range check by casting the upper bound to a signed long. This prevents a warning that was incorrectly triggered by negative offsets, ensuring the range check behaves correctly.

The commit removes an unnecessary case statement (HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD) from the SetHwReg8188EU() function in the 8188EU driver. The case previously only called a helper function, so the commit replaces the case with a direct call to that helper and deletes the case. This is a code cleanup to eliminate redundant HAL layer code.

The commit changes the linkage of several symbols in arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c from external to static, addressing sparse warnings that were introduced during errata additions. This improves code quality by preventing accidental external linkage and potential future bugs.

The commit removes an incorrect "interrupt flags" cell from the MDSS device tree bindings for Qualcomm MSM displays. The MDSS interrupt provider is a single cell, so specifying flags on consumers is invalid. The change cleans up the binding definition to prevent misuse.

The commit changes the V4L2 controls subsystem to allocate memory for arrays inside the struct ctrl_ref, enabling drivers to specify variable array sizes instead of using a fixed size. This adds new functionality for dynamic array handling.

This commit extends the Qualcomm socinfo driver by adding support for several new System-on-Chips (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, an additional SM8450) and associated PMICs. The changes involve adding new entries to the driver’s data structures so that these devices can be identified and reported correctly.

The commit updates the rtl8xxxu Wi-Fi driver to correctly recognize all possible chip cut identifiers (letters A through P). Previously, the driver only handled cuts A–E and printed "unknown" for any higher value, which could lead to misidentification or incorrect behavior. This change expands the recognition logic to cover the full range of valid chip cuts.

This commit removes a custom software enable counter field (.is_enabled) from the PRCMU clock structures in the ux500 clock driver. The core clock framework already tracks enable counts, so the redundant field is dropped to simplify the code and avoid maintaining duplicate state.

The commit reorganises the RISC‑V memory‑management initialisation code to minimise the use of the CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL preprocessor guard. It moves the pt_ops macro into a dedicated #ifdef block and groups together several macro‑only declarations that were previously scattered, thereby reducing conditional compilation complexity.

The commit adds device tree entries for the QUP (Qualcomm Universal Peripheral) and GPI (Generic Peripheral Interface) DMA controllers on the sm6350-lena platform. This enables the kernel to use these hardware blocks for I2C communication, paving the way for functional I2C nodes to be added later.
The commit updates comments in the thermal driver for Renesas RZG2L. It replaces a vague comment, corrects a typo, and adjusts comment formatting to match coding style.

The commit fixes an infinite loop in the release_swfw_sync routine of the Intel Graphics driver (igc). The loop occurs when the code fails to acquire a hardware semaphore required for resource release, typically after a surprise hardware removal. The patch adds a check for semaphore acquisition failure, logs an error, and exits the loop instead of spinning indefinitely.

The commit updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to replace legacy bus clock definitions with the newer ASoC DAI format terminology, removing old style defines and aligning the driver with modern clocking conventions.
The commit removes a redundant IS_SWAPFILE macro check from the NFS code. Since IS_SWAPFILE is always defined, the check serves no purpose and is eliminated to simplify the code.

The commit adds handling for GTP echo requests in the GTP kernel driver. When a GTP_ECHO_REQ packet is received, the driver now sends back a GTP_ECHO_RSP packet, including the GTPIE_RECOVERY information element and an appropriate restart counter (0 for GTPv1, a value from userspace for GTPv0). This implements a missing feature required by the GTP specification.

This commit replaces calls to cpumask_next_* with cpumask_first_* where the start index is -1 (i.e., start at 0), which is a more efficient and correct analogue. It also introduces a new helper function cpumask_first_zero() to provide functionality that was previously missing. The change improves correctness and performance by using the appropriate function and adds a new feature to the cpumask API.

The commit removes the call to the firmware IOVAR "dtim_assoc" in the brcmfmac driver. This call is no longer supported by the firmware and always fails, producing a repeated error message on the console after a STA roams. By eliminating the call, the driver no longer attempts to retrieve unsupported TIM information, thereby stopping the error output.

This commit adds a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) for the Intel ASoC driver sof_rt5682, enabling support for the alc5682 headset codec on platforms that lack a speaker amplifier. It connects the codec to SSP0 following the Intel Bluetooth offload design, thereby extending the driver’s hardware support.

The commit adds a check in the io_uring net subsystem to return an error when a zerocopy sendmsg operation is requested on a socket that does not support zerocopy. This mirrors a previous change that handled zerocopy send requests, but the sendmsg path was missing the failure logic, potentially allowing unsupported zerocopy sendmsg calls to succeed and later cause failures.

The commit updates the kernel documentation preamble to conditionally load the xeCJK package only if the xeCJK.sty file is present. This prevents a build failure on systems where the texlive-xecjk package is installed without its dependencies, ensuring that "make pdfdocs" works for non-CJK content even if texlive-xecjk is missing.

This commit modifies the checkpatch.pl script to suppress a specific style warning that incorrectly flags macros containing the keyword "case". The change updates the script’s parsing logic so that macros like "#define C2S(x) case x: return #x" are no longer reported as errors, acknowledging that such macros are legitimate in many in-kernel drivers.

The commit replaces the simple-KMS helper functions in the DRM simpledrm driver with the standard atomic helpers. It removes empty CRTC atomic enable/disable helpers and ensures drm_atomic_helper_check_plane_state() is always called. No functional behavior changes are introduced; the change is purely a refactor to use a better‑architected helper set for easier code sharing.
The commit introduces a new JPEG decoding worker interface in the MediaTek JPEG decoder driver, enabling three hardware decoders to run concurrently on the MT8195 platform.

The commit removes VPRINTK() debug macros and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper from libata. These calls were redundant because tracepoints already provide the same information, so the change cleans up unnecessary code.

The commit adds support for entering zstate during the extended vblank period when the display is in FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO. It calculates the extra time available from the added vblank lines, allows zstate entry only if this time exceeds 5 ms, writes an optimized value for min_dst_y_next_start to a register, and restores defaults when exiting freesync.

The commit corrects the type of the third parameter in the hqd_destroy() callback function pointer prototype from an enum to a uint32_t, matching the actual implementations. This change eliminates a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure observed when booting a kernel compiled with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG on an RX 6700 XT.

The commit corrects the property name for the reset binding in the STM32 pinctrl device tree bindings from the incorrect 'reset' to the correct 'resets', and adds an 'additionalProperties: false' constraint to the binding schema to prevent future misuse.
The commit adds a compiler directive to silence a GCC 12 array-bounds warning in the ath9k driver. It does not change functionality, only suppresses a false-positive warning.
This commit changes the sun8i-ss crypto driver to use pm_runtime_resume_and_get instead of pm_runtime_get_sync, simplifying the runtime PM handling.

The commit reorganizes io_uring code by moving the fixed file installation and removal logic into the filetable module, where it logically belongs. It also changes helper functions to accept a context structure instead of an io_kiocb, reflecting that these helpers are not tied to a specific request. The change is purely a refactor to improve code structure and clarity, with no new functionality or bug fixes.

This commit updates the device tree bindings for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU to support separate G1 and G2 decoder nodes. It adds new bindings that allow each decoder block to be independently controlled via the vpu-blk-ctrl power-domain controller, which now has a proper driver. The change enables proper configuration of the two decoders as distinct nodes in the device tree.

The commit removes the macro IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON, which was only used once inside the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro, and inlines its contents directly into IWL_DEVICE_BZ. This is a refactoring that cleans up an unused macro and simplifies the code.
